name: Bharwa Lal Mirch Ka Achar Recipe (Stuffed Red Chilli Pickle)

cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "8 oz (80 g) fennel seeds (saunf) 4.4 oz (125 g) fenugreek seeds (methi seeds) 1.4 oz (40 g) nigella seeds (kalaunji) 3.5 oz (100 g) brown mustard seeds (kali sarson) 3.5 oz (100 g) Kashmiri red chilli powder 3.5 oz (100 g) turmeric powder 8 oz (225 g) salt 1.4 oz (40 g) dry mango powder (amchoor powder) to a grinder and grind to make a coarse powder. Note – If your grinder is not large enough, powder the ingredients separately and mix them all later. Wash 3 pounds (1.5 kg) of large red chilies and wipe each of them using a kitchen towel. Note – If there is some leftover masala, then add it to the jar over the chilies."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Add 1.5 liters of mustard oil to a pan and bring it to a boil over medium heat. Remove the pan from heat and let the oil cool down completely."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Boiling the oil is important to remove any moisture in it and mellow down the pungent mustard flavor."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Pour the cooled oil over the stuffed chilies in the jar. Keep it in the sun for another 10-12 days."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Once the red chilli pickle is ready, move the jar into your kitchen. Store this pickle for up to a year at room temperate in a cool and dry place."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Add fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, nigella seeds, mustard seeds, Kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt, and dry mango powder to a grinder and grind to make a coarse powder."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Note – If your grinder is not large enough, then powder the ingredients separately and mix them all together later."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Wash the chilies and wipe each of them using a kitchen towel."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Note – If there is some leftover masala, then just add it in the jar over the chilies."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Add mustard oil to a pan and bring it to a boil over medium heat."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Remove the pan from heat and let the oil cool down completely."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Boiling the oil is important to get rid of any moisture in it and also to mellow down the pungent mustard flavor."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Pour the cooled oil over the stuffed chilies in the jar. The chilies must be covered with oil. If they are not covered, then heat some more oil and add it to the jar."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Now remove the cloth and secure the lid tightly. name: Maharashtrian Puran Poli Sweet Recipe
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "1 cup Bengal gram lentil (chana dal)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "1 cup grated jaggery", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "1 tablespoon ghee", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon nutmeg powder", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon cardamom powder", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "2 cups whole wheat flour (gehu ka atta)", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "½ cup all-purpose flour (maida)", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon turmeric powder (optional)", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon salt", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons hot ghee (plus more for frying)", "source_position": 10}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Wash the dal with water 2-3 times and soak it in 4-5 cups of water for 30 minutes."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Once the dal is soaked, drain the water and add the soaked chana dal to a pressure cooker."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Add 3 cups of water to the cooker and close the lid. Pressure cook the dal on high heat for one whistle, then reduce the heat to low and cook for another 15 minutes."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Remove the cooker from heat and let the pressure release naturally. Once the pressure is released, open the lid of the cooker."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "The dal must be well-cooked. If it’s not cooked well, cook it for another few minutes."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Once the dal is soaked, drain the water and add the soaked chana dal to an instant pot."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add 3 cups of water and close the lid of the instant pot."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Press PRESSURE COOK and set the timer to 15 minutes on high pressure."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Once the timer goes off, let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Release the remaining pressure manually and open the lid of the instant pot."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Strain the cooked dal using a fine-mesh strainer and reserve the water for other uses like making katachi amti or curries. Try to get rid of as much liquid as possible while straining."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add the cooked dal, jaggery, and ghee to a nonstick pan and cook on medium heat until the jaggery is dissolved and mixed with the dal."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Add nutmeg powder and cardamom powder and mix well."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Cook the mixture until it starts leaving the pan’s sides and is thickened nicely (12-15 minutes). Stir frequently and keep mashing the dal with the back of the spoon."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Note – Insert a spoon in the middle to check if the filling is cooked well. If it can stand straight, then the puran is cooked to the right consistency."}, {"step_number": 16, "instruction": "Remove the filling from the heat and let it cool down for an hour."}, {"step_number": 17, "instruction": "Add the cooled puran to a blender or a food processor and blend until it is very smooth."}, {"step_number": 18, "instruction": "You can also mash the puran with the heels of your hands until very smooth."}, {"step_number": 19, "instruction": "Note – The idea is to make a very smooth pooran. If the puran is not smooth, the poli will tear while rolling."}, {"step_number": 20, "instruction": "Note – A particular device called puran yantra is used in many Maharashtrian houses to make the puran super smooth. Some people also pass it through a mesh strainer."}, {"step_number": 21, "instruction": "Add whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, salt, turmeric powder, and hot ghee to a large mixing bowl (or a parat) and mix well using your fingertips."}, {"step_number": 22, "instruction": "Gradually add water (approx 1 cup) and knead to make a very soft dough. The quantity of water will depend on the quality of the flour used. Do not add much water at once; otherwise, the dough will become sticky."}, {"step_number": 23, "instruction": "Tip – You can use your stand mixer fitted with a dough attachment or a food processer to knead the dough."}, {"step_number": 24, "instruction": "Cover the dough with a damp kitchen towel and keep it aside for 30 minutes."}, {"step_number": 25, "instruction": "Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions and roll each piece to make smooth balls."}, {"step_number": 26, "instruction": "Similarly, divide the puran into 6-8 equal portions and roll to make balls."}, {"step_number": 27, "instruction": "Take one of the portions of the dough and slightly flatten the ball between your palms."}, {"step_number": 28, "instruction": "Dust it with dry flour gently and roll to make a 4-5 inch circle."}, {"step_number": 29, "instruction": "Keep a filling ball in the circle’s center and bring the ends together."}, {"step_number": 30, "instruction": "Roll the filled dough ball in between your palms and keep rotating while rolling. This step will ensure that the puran reaches the corners of the poli."}, {"step_number": 31, "instruction": "Now keep the disc on the rolling surface. Dust with dry flour and roll using a rolling pin to make an 8-10 inch circle. While rolling, apply the pressure gently and go from one side to the other. Try to roll the edges a little thinner than the center. Keep rotating the surface if it is getting tough to roll from one side."}, {"step_number": 32, "instruction": "Heat a griddle (tawa) over medium-high heat."}, {"step_number": 33, "instruction": "Once the tawa is hot, transfer the puran poli to it."}, {"step_number": 34, "instruction": "Cook until brown spots appear on the bottom side. Flip the poli and cook until brown spots appear on the other side."}, {"step_number": 35, "instruction": "Flip the poli again and apply 1 teaspoon of ghee on each side. Press the poli using the back of a spoon and cook until golden brown from both sides."}, {"step_number": 36, "instruction": "Make all the poli in the same manner. Apply some more ghee and serve it with milk or katachi amti."}, {"step_number": 37, "instruction": "Note – Wipe the skillet every time before adding the next poli."}]

name: Mango Shrikhand Recipe (Amrakhand)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "1 pound plain yogurt (dahi, curd) (500 g, or Greek yogurt)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "½ cup mango puree (homemade or canned)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon ground cardamom", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "1 pinch saffron strands", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "¼ cup powdered sugar", "source_position": 5}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "How to make mango puree? To make mango puree, wash a large, ripe, and juicy mango and wipe it with a kitchen cloth. Peel the skin using a sharp knife. Cut the flesh away from the seed and discard the seed. Chop the mango flesh into pieces. Add the pieces to a blender and blend until smooth. Mango puree is ready. You can refrigerate it for 3-4 days or freeze it for 6 months. Check out my detailed post on how to make mango puree here. Can I replace mango with anything else? Replace mango puree with strawberry puree or blueberry puree and make strawberry shrikhand or blueberry shrikhand."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Make Hung Yogurt Keep a fine-mesh strainer over a large bowl and line the strainer with a muslin cloth. Transfer yogurt to the strainer, gather the cloth's corners, and tie it into a bundle. Tip – Keep a heavy object (like a stone mortar on top of a wooden round plate) over the yogurt. It will make the dripping process quicker. Keep the bowl in the refrigerator for 12-18 hours. If using Greek yogurt, you can reduce the time to 6-7 hours. The liquid will drip from the yogurt making it thick and creamy. The thick yogurt that is collected now is called CHAKKA. Discard the liquid or drink it."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Transfer the thickened yogurt to a medium mixing bowl and whisk well until smooth and creamy."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add mango puree, cardamom, saffron strands, and sugar to the bowl and mix well until combined."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Check for sugar and add more if needed."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Refrigerate the mango shrikhand for a few hours. Garnish with slivered dry fruits and nuts. Serve chilled."}]

name: Easy Sabudana Kheer Recipe (Indian Tapioca Pudding)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "½ cup medium size sabudana (sago, tapioca pearls)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "½ cup water (for soaking)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "34 ounces whole milk (full-fat milk) (1 liter)", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "15-20 strands saffron (optional)", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon ground cardamom (elaichi powder)", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "¼ cup granulated white sugar (or to taste)", "source_position": 6}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Note – Unlike the sabudana khichdi recipe where you have to rinse sabudana many times to get rid of the excess starch, sabudana kheer recipe requires you to wash it just once. We want the starch to make the kheer creamy and luscious."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Soak sabudana in ½ cup water for 4-5 hours. Tip – If you have forgotten to soak the sabudana beforehand, then soak it in boiling water for 30 minutes."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Heat 34 oz (1 liter) whole milk over medium-high heat in a heavy-bottom pan. Stir frequently to prevent scorching at the bottom of the pan. When the milk boils, stir in the soaked sabudana along with 15-20 saffron strands. Note – Never add the sabudana to the pan before the milk comes to a rolling boil. Doing so might curdle the milk. Reduce the heat to low and cook the kheer until it gets thick and the sabudana is cooked well (Approx 40-60 minutes). The consistency of the ready kheer should be flowy like custard. Stir very frequently while cooking. Post-cooking, sabudana pearls must be soft, slightly sticky, and translucent. There shouldn’t be any bite in them."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Stir in ¼ cup of granulated sugar and ½ teaspoon cardamom powder and cook for another 2-3 minutes. Check for sugar and add more if you like the kheer sweeter. Garnish with slivered almonds and pistachios and dried rose petals. Serve sago kheer hot or chilled."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Rinse ½ cup sabudana with water and soak it in ½ cup water for 4-5 hours."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Add the soaked sabudana, ½ cup water, and 15-20 strands of saffron to the instant pot and stir gently. name: Easy Sweet Shakarpara Recipe (Shakkar Pare)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "2 cups all purpose flour (maida) (250 g, 8.5 oz)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons ghee (room temperature)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "lukewarm water", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "3 cups ghee or oil (for frying)", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "1 cup granulated white sugar", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "½ cup water", "source_position": 6}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Add all-purpose flour and ghee in a large mixing bowl or parat (large shallow plate) and mix well using your hands. The ghee should be at room temperature and in a semi-solid state. If it’s very warm where you live and the ghee is melted at room temperature, then refrigerate it for a few minutes."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Add lukewarm water (approx ½ cup) little by little and knead to make a tight dough."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Cover the dough with a dishcloth and keep aside for 15 minutes."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Knead the dough once again for 20-30 seconds and divide it into 2 parts."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Roll one part of the dough to make a circle. The thickness of the circle should be approximately ¼ inch. Do not roll it thin otherwise, the shakarpara will turn out hard."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Do not use dry flour to roll. Since the dough is hard and has fat in it, it will not stick to the surface."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Note - If your dough is still sticking to the surface while rolling, then use very little dry flour."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Keep the remaining dough covered with a cloth to avoid drying."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Cut the circle into small square or diamond shapes using a sharp knife."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Just before you start to roll the dough, heat 3 cups of ghee or oil for frying in a pan over medium flame."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Once the ghee is hot, reduce the heat to medium-low."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "To check if the ghee is hot, drop a small ball of dough into the hot ghee. It should rise on top in 5-8 seconds. If not, then heat the ghee a little more. If the ghee is too hot, the dough ball will come to the top immediately. In this case, switch off the heat and let the ghee cool down for 5 minutes before switching on the heat again."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Add the shakarpare to the hot ghee and fry on medium-low heat until lightly browned. Stir frequently while frying."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Do not get tempted and increase the heat. Each batch will take 20-25 minutes to fry properly. Fry only until the shakarpara are light brown in color. They will not look and taste good if the color changes to dark brown."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Drain on a plate lined with paper towels and let them cool completely."}, {"step_number": 16, "instruction": "Roll, cut, and fry the other part of the dough in the same manner."}, {"step_number": 17, "instruction": "While the shakarpara are cooling down, make the sugar syrup."}, {"step_number": 18, "instruction": "Add granulated sugar and water to a pan and heat on medium heat for 10-12 minutes to make 2-3 thread syrup."}, {"step_number": 19, "instruction": "The consistency of the sugar syrup is very important for it to crystallize properly over the shakarpara."}, {"step_number": 20, "instruction": "To check if the sugar syrup has reached the 2-3 thread consistency, take some syrup on a spoon. Let it cool for 4-5 seconds. Be careful because the syrup is very hot at this stage. Now take a little syrup on one of your fingers and press it with the thumb. Pull the finger away from the thumb. You should see 2-3 threads forming between the finger and the thumb."}, {"step_number": 21, "instruction": "If you have a candy thermometer, then the temperature of the syrup should be 242°F–248°F (116°C–120°C)."}, {"step_number": 22, "instruction": "Once the right consistency of the sugar syrup is reached, remove the pan from heat."}, {"step_number": 23, "instruction": "Add the cooled shakarpare to the pan with sugar syrup immediately and mix gently to coat them with the syrup. The syrup will start to crystallize almost immediately after removing the pan from the heat so be a little quick with this step."}, {"step_number": 24, "instruction": "Separate the shakarpara using your fingers and let them rest for 10 minutes."}, {"step_number": 25, "instruction": "Store them in an airtight container for up to a month at room temperature."}]

name: Shankarpali Recipe
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "240 grams all-purpose flour (maida)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "20 grams fine semolina (barik sooji)", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "50 grams ghee", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "100 grams sugar powder", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "¼ cup milk (approximately) (at room temperature)", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "oil or ghee (for frying)", "source_position": 6}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Add the following ingredients to a large mixing bowl or parat (the wide, shallow plate used in Indian cooking). Mix well using your fingers until combined."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "g of all-purpose flour (maida) 20 g of fine semolina (barik sooji)"}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Heat 50 g ghee in a small pan over high heat until very hot, almost smoking."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Pour the hot, melted ghee over the flour mixture. First, use a spoon, then your fingertips, to rub the hot ghee into the flour with a crumbling motion. After mixing, take a fistful of the mixture and press it in your palm. It should hold its shape and not crumble. If it does not hold, the moyan is insufficient; add another teaspoon of hot melted ghee. This process of adding ghee to the flour is called Moyan. It makes the shankarpali crispy and flaky."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Pro Tip: Hot ghee penetrates the flour particles more effectively than warm or room-temperature ghee, coating each one with fat. This fat barrier prevents gluten from forming excessively, which is what creates those beautiful, flaky, melt-in-the-mouth layers."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Now add 100 g sugar powder and mix well. Powdering your sugar at home is easy. Blitz regular granulated sugar in a mixer for 30 seconds."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add milk (approximately ¼ cup), little by little, to the flour mixture and knead into a firm, tight dough, similar in consistency to poori dough. The dough should not be soft or sticky. If it feels too soft, it will absorb oil during frying, making it greasy and dense."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Cover the dough with a dishcloth and keep it aside for 15 minutes. This resting time allows the gluten to relax and the fat to distribute evenly, making the dough easier to roll. It is an important step; do not skip it."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Add all-purpose flour and semolina to a large bowl or parat (a large shallow Indian plate). Mix well using your fingers until combined."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Heat the ghee in a small pan over high heat until very hot, almost smoking."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Pour the hot, melted ghee over the flour mixture. Using a spoon first, and then your fingertips, rub the hot ghee into the flour using a crumbling motion. After mixing, take a fistful of the mixture and press it in your palm. It should hold its shape and not crumble. If it does not hold, the moyan is insufficient; add another teaspoon of melted ghee. This process of adding ghee to the flour is called Moyan. It makes the shankarpali crispy and flaky."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add sugar powder and mix well."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Add milk, little by little, to the flour mixture and knead into a firm, tight dough, similar to poori dough. The dough should not be soft or sticky. If it feels too soft, it will absorb oil during frying and turn greasy and dense."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Knead the dough for 20-30 seconds, then divide it into 3 parts."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Using a sharp knife, pizza cutter, or a karanji cutter , cut the rolled dough into diamond shapes (make diagonal cuts in two directions) or squares, each roughly 1 inch in size. Remove the uneven ends."}, {"step_number": 16, "instruction": "Heat oil or ghee for frying in a pan over medium heat. I prefer using ghee."}, {"step_number": 17, "instruction": "Once the ghee is hot, reduce the heat to medium-low."}, {"step_number": 18, "instruction": "Add the squares to hot ghee and fry on medium-low heat until golden brown. Use a slotted spatula to stir frequently while frying to ensure even browning. Each batch will take 12-15 minutes to fry."}, {"step_number": 19, "instruction": "Drain on a plate lined with paper towels. Shankarpali will not be crispy at this stage. They will crisp up once cooled."}, {"step_number": 20, "instruction": "Repeat the process with the remaining dough. Make sure to check the ghee’s temperature before adding the next batch."}]

name: Thandai Phirni Recipe
